commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Canvas tool symlink escape test from a hardcoded Windows skip to an import-time file-symlink capability probe.

PR surface: Tests +16. Total +16 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and v2026.6.1 both skip the Canvas jsonlPath symlink regression on every Windows host. The positive symlink-capable Windows path is not proven by the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-supplied symlink-capable Windows proof is needed before merge; ClawSweeper cannot repair that environment proof gap in the repository.

target.title: test: detect file symlink support dynamically in canvas tool test
target.body:
Replaces the hardcoded Windows skip with a dynamic file symlink capability check, allowing the test to run on Windows systems when Developer Mode or symlink privileges are available.

### Verified Windows Proof

The following test execution log shows the test run on Windows. Since symlink privileges are not present on this host, the symlink test was skipped gracefully as expected (1 skipped, 2 passed), rather than failing the whole suite:

```
 RUN  v4.1.7 C:/Users/ANIRUDDHA/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/openclaw

 ✓  extensions  ../../extensions/canvas/src/tool.test.ts (3 tests | 1 skipped) 211ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 1 skipped (3)
```
target.author: aniruddhaadak80
